raw_consumer_statement:
In XXXXXXXX XXXX I paid a {$320.00} annual fee for my American Express credit card ( ending with XXXX ). Within approximately one month of paying this annual fee, American Express cancelled my account without providing adequate explanation for the closure.

I immediately contacted American Express customer service to request a refund of the annual fee since my account was closed so shortly after I had paid it. The customer service representative agreed to process my refund request, and I subsequently received a refund check for the {$320.00} annual fee.

However, after receiving the refund check, a new charge appeared on my account labeled " REMOVAL OF CREDIT BALANCE. '' American Express is holding me responsible for this charge, but when I contacted customer service, they could not provide a clear explanation of what this charge represents or why it was applied to my account.

The sequence of events creates confusion : I paid an annual fee, my account was quickly cancelled, I received a refund for that fee, but then a mysterious charge appeared that seems related to the refund process. The charge description " REMOVAL OF CREDIT BALANCE '' is vague and uninformative. I have received no written explanation or prior notification about this charge.
